title: Smoky Flaky Kenyan Floral Hash
description: A lacto-vegetarian-friendly Kenyan dish featuring a smoky, flaky texture and a floral aroma, perfect for a brunch gathering. The liquid cheese adds a rich and creamy element to the dish.
instructions: Preheat a ttukbaegi earthenware cook to 400°F (200°C).
Prepare the ingredients by peeling and chopping the sweet potatoes into 1-inch pieces, and mincing the onion and garlic.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the chopped sweet potatoes, onion, garlic, dried rose petals, and liquid cheese. Mix well to combine.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in the preheated earthenware cook over medium heat. Add the sweet potato mixture and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil, salt, and black pepper to the earthenware cook. Stir well to combine.
Transfer the earthenware cook to a hot stone plate and garnish with fresh parsley. Serve immediately, with hot sauce on the side if desired.
Serve the Smoky Flaky Kenyan Floral Hash hot, garnished with additional fresh parsley if desired.
